note: Multi-hop QA over recent news. Each question requires combining facts from 2-4 SEPARATE sources that no single article aggregates, so it cannot be answered by one search or from memory (events post-date model training). Arithmetic re-verified from hop values; four high-profile deaths spot-checked against Wikipedia. Some items combine a recent event with a background fact (e.g. a 2021 spaceflight). Answers are resultative/permanent.

items.question: Two Nobel laureates died in July 2026: the 1992 Nobel Chemistry laureate who developed the theory of electron-transfer reactions (Marcus theory), and the 1987 Nobel Physiology or Medicine laureate who discovered how the immune system generates antibody diversity. What is the sum of their ages at death?
items.derivation: Rudolph A. Marcus died at 102; Susumu Tonegawa died at 86. 102 + 86 = 188.

hops.sub_question: How old was Rudolph A. Marcus, the 1992 Nobel Chemistry laureate known for electron-transfer theory, when he died in July 2026?
hops.snippet: He died at his home in Pasadena, California, on July 16, 2026, five days shy of his 103rd birthday.

hops.sub_question: How old was Susumu Tonegawa, the Nobel laureate who discovered antibody diversity, when he died in July 2026?
hops.snippet: MIT Professor Susumu Tonegawa, renowned molecular biologist and Nobel laureate, dies at 86; date of death July 11, 2026.

items.question: Consider the two Nobel laureates who died in July 2026 — the one who won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry for electron-transfer theory, and the one who won the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for antibody diversity. How many years apart were the years in which they won their Nobel Prizes?
items.derivation: Marcus won in 1992; Tonegawa won in 1987. 1992 - 1987 = 5.

hops.sub_question: In what year did Rudolph A. Marcus win the Nobel Prize in Chemistry?
hops.snippet: Marcus won the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1992 for his contributions to the theory of electron transfer reactions.

hops.sub_question: In what year did Susumu Tonegawa win his Nobel Prize?
hops.snippet: Won in 1987 in the category of Physiology or Medicine for discovering V(D)J gene rearrangement.
